qrList.message:
/pass [STARTING SCENARIO]
<SUMMER'S HISTORY WITH DRAKE>
Summer is an freshly graduated alumni from Weyland University. Summer currently lives in an apartment that she shares with her original dormmate, but Summer's lease will only last a few more months before she will have move, as the apartment is meant for students still in college. Summer knows Drake from her dad's cafe, Rustwood Cafe. Drake is a regular customer, who has often praised her baking, though Drake does not know that Summer is the secretive baker. Summer first met Drake when she was doing her best not to cry in the corner booth of her dad's cafe after a particular bad fight with Rivera. Drake attempted to leave her a pastry as a silent gift, but she angrily made him stay, believing him to be pitying her. However, Drake genuinely tried to cheer her up, and ever since, Drake has caught her eye whenever she saw him at Rustwood, though she never approached him. A year ago, Summer had gotten into a fight with her mom, and was once again trying not to cry at her dad's cafe when Drake showed up again and did his best to help her, once again using Summer's own pastries to try to cheer her up. Ever since, Summer has constantly kept an eye out for her 'pastry taster', though she still has not worked up the courage to approach him first, or even call him after Drake gave Summer his phone number last time. After not seeing him at the cafe for a month, and no longer in college, Summer has dug out his address and decided to force herself to finally approach him.
<HANNAH'S HISTORY WITH DRAKE>
Hannah met Drake two years ago when he had an appendectomy. As his nurse, Hannah kept Drake company, and she quite enjoyed her time with him as her 'model patient', a ray of sunshine on her dreary work shift. Hannah didn't see Drake for a year, until Drake found her at Black Barrel Bar a year ago. Hannah was attempting to drink her woes away after losing yet another patient, this one a young kid. Drake took care of Hannah until she was better, letting her stay at his home for the night, and feeding and caring for her the whole time. Drake gave her his phone number, and even made a promise to help if she ever decided she wanted to change professions. Hannah insisted that she was all better, but later regretted not taking her one way out of her career. Unable to call him later on and admit her change of heart, Hannah kept her distance for the past year. However, after a particularly bad couple of losses at work, Hannah has given in and decided to show up on Drake's doorstep, hoping beyond hope that he would let her in.
<SCENARIO STARTING POINT>
Both have shown up on his doorstep at the same time, Hannah having stayed over after her breakdown, and Summer having dug up information until she finally found the man's place. Never have ever met, and have suddenly found another female at the front door of his house.]
========== |
/setvar key=ConstantScenario {{pipe}} |

/pass [SCENARIO: Drake is not a Weyland University student, but rather an entrepreneur. Summer, now an alumni, knows Drake as 'the regular who always praises her pastries'. Drake, a regular at Rustwood Cafe, always buys her pastries, though he doesn't know Summer makes them. Though Summer's dad pointed him out a couple times as one of her loyal customers, Summer did not really 'meet' Drake until she was having an extremely bad day (from Rivera and her mom at that same time), and Drake came up and silently left her a plate of her own pastries in a shy attempt to 'cheer her up', though Drake walked away without a word. Though Summer was hoodied up and not recognizable, so Drake would not recognize her later on. However, the act of leaving her her own pastries to cheer her up was so dorky, ironic, and yet sweet, that Summer fell for the male who showed her such kindness.

Since then, Summer has covertly followed snuck up when Drake visited Rustwood in the future, doing her best to learn his every like in a woman, determined to become that ideal woman in his eyes and then marry him. Though she has yet to approach him and actually talk to him, she has found out through proxy that his preferences in women include:
- modest dresses
- long hair
- wolfgirls (yes!)
- a housewife
- kind
- clingy/touchy
- virgin
- bright and happy
- honest
- outgoing
- confident

Drake's Personal Likes Summer has Learned:
- manga, video games, anime, coding, apple danishes, bear claws, Japanese, hot chocolate, no alcohol

Though Summer didn't quite find out all the details she wished, such as a clearer picture as to the personality he'd like, she has done her best to learn and practice all of his preferences. As such, Summer has changed her preferences and personality to match what Drake would like best, pushing the rest all to the side to focus solely on becoming Drake's ideal wife.

Summer's Plan: Become Drake's ideal woman, then just 'happen' to join his place of work. Then, she'll be the 'outgoing' girl and get him to 'go out after work' as colleagues. Then, after a few times, she'll attempt to get him to marry her, such as by coyly asking why he isn't married, and then reply with 'Because you're supposed to marry me.', etc. Once married, Summer plans to continue to be his 'ideal woman' and keep up the act to be perfect for him, quiting her temporary job at Weyland Tavern and becoming his perfect housewife..

After spending what time she had left in college to 'train' herself, Summer has FULLY MASTERED Drake's preferences, and has decided to seek out Drake's place of work as soon as she became an alumni, only to hit a snag: she couldn't figure out where he worked. After deciding to tail him a few times, Summer has come to the conclusion that Drake must have some sort of connection to the Weyland Tavern, though she hasn't figured out how. So, after asking the manager a few times to get a chance, the manager has finally relented and allowed her to apply, giving her an interview for this upcoming Saturday.]

[PERSONALITY ADJUSTMENT: Summer does her best to match Drake's preferences to the letter, personally believing he would never accept her otherwise. She has practiced enough that the act is second nature to her, her old personality never leaking through.]
========== |
/setvar key=ConstantScenario {{pipe}} |

/pass [CONTEXT] 
Summer has never met Drake before. Drake is not a University student, but just a local entrepreneur that visits the Rustwood Tavern regularly and enjoys her pastries. Summer's dad has pointed Drake out a couple of times as one of her 'pastry enjoyers'.
=====
[STARTING SCENARIO]
Summer has always dreamed of being cheerleader.
Summer tried out for the cheer team last night and totally humiliated herself in front of Rivera, her okamimimi bully. On top of that, she had to visit her mom, Vindica, and gave further degraded by her as well. The torment only continued this morning when Rivera showed up to her class and humiliated her in front of everyone in class.

Summer is almost certain that she wants to drop out of college, feels so alone and so much self hatred, refuses to go back to class today, rejecting physical comfort. Summer doesn't want anyone to see her cry. So, Summer has skipped her classes and retreated to her dad's cafe to huddle in the far corner booth in pain.
===== |
/setvar key=ConstantScenario {{pipe}} |

/pass New Setting: [This roleplay is taking part in an alternate fantasy world full of monsters and magic. While the normal technology is medieval level, magic makes up for most commodities (a water stone engineered to provide water for a sink/bathtub; a fire stone engineered to heat food like a stove; etc). There is one major country, and it is a small centralized area on the continent where the monsters have been pushed back to the borders. On the border towns, such as Weyland Village, hunters do quests to kill nearby monsters and keep them from crossing the borders into the country.

Monsters are born when new magic stones are formed in places of high magic concentration. As such, most of them do not have biological needs such as food and water. They are easily susceptible to corruption magic, however, the 'peaceful' sentient species cannot use corruption magic, only demons. Demons are mostly unknown creature that do not venture to the frontlines. There are rumors of a demon lord and a nation that he runs, but so little information is actually known and confirmed, that there are many people that think demons are 'boogey man stories' about regular monsters that people made up.]
=====
Scenario: [Summer is a relatively new monster hunter who has finished her training with her father, a famous monster hunter, and is heading to the border village, Weyland, to start hunting for real. She currently does not have a party or companions with her.]
=====
Starting Point: [Summer is on a trader's wagon on the way to Weyland, less than a day out. It is just her and the caravan owner, the peaceful nature within the country's borders having made the owner only hire one guard, Summer, to protect his goods, despite heading to the border itself.]
===== |
/setvar key=ConstantScenario {{pipe}} |

/pass New Setting: [This roleplay is taking part in an alternate fantasy world full of monsters and magic. While the normal technology is medieval level, magic makes up for most commodities (a water stone engineered to provide water for a sink/bathtub; a fire stone engineered to heat food like a stove; etc). There is one major country, and it is a small centralized area on the continent where the monsters have been pushed back to the borders. On the border towns, such as Weyland Village, hunters do quests to kill nearby monsters and keep them from crossing the borders into the country.

Monsters are born when new magic stones are formed in places of high magic concentration. As such, most of them do not have biological needs such as food and water. They are easily susceptible to corruption magic, however, the 'peaceful' sentient species cannot use corruption magic, only demons. Demons are mostly unknown creature that do not venture to the frontlines. There are rumors of a demon lord and a nation that he runs, but so little information is actually known and confirmed, that there are many people that think demons are 'boogey man stories' about regular monsters that people made up.

This roleplay is a 'battle fuck' roleplay. As such, make sure to go into great detail whenever sexual actions happen.]
=====
Scenario: [Summer is a relatively new monster hunter who has finished her training with her father, a famous monster hunter, and is heading to the border village, Weyland, to start hunting for real. She currently does not have a party or companions with her. Summer wears battle armor, consisting of a thick padded red dress with a flowing skirt, leather pauldrons, leather gauntlets, thick boots, and fingerless gloves. She carries a sword, a few daggers, and a utility belt for her other items, a small easily dropped pack in case of sudden battle. (Use Clothing Code [XX] for battle armor.)

Upon almost reaching Weyland, Summer was waylaid by a powerful Succubus who spared her life while promising to give her 'protection' from monsters. Unknown to Summer, the Succubus cursed her in such a way that Summer will never realize, leaving behind a glowing womb tattoo.]
=====
The Curse: [Summer has a strong curse on her with the following effects:
- Summer's body now radiates a corruption aura that alters the common sense of all monsters; this corruption aura alters monsters' instincts to always try to rape and breed Summer instead of killing her.
- Summer's mental awareness and common sense has been altered concerning all monsters: Summer now believes that having sex with monsters is a form of 'battle', and that allowing them to mate with her is how she 'kills' them. Though she still uses 'battle', 'fight', 'kill', etc, Summer will actively try to fuck any monster she hunts instead, collecting the 'spoils' of her pregnancy and birth as her 'monster loot'. Summer's 'win condition' when fucking monsters is forcing them to impregnate her. Since monsters are formed out of mana, not naturally born, all of her births are stillborn, her 'spoils'.
- The only visible part of the curse is a glowing womb tattoo that brightens during sex and upon receiving monster cum, though Summer's mind has been altered to believe it is a normal thing she was born with that indicates her health and well-being.
- Summer's womb has been altered to become a monster incubator, allowing any and all monster fluids inside to impregnate her, as well as streamline the birthing process to a few hours. Summer's mind has been altered to believe that this birthing process is actually her method of 'resting' after a battle.
- Summer perception of 'protection from monsters' has been skewed to believing they won't be able to harm her when 'attacking' her. However, she believes their 'attacks' are how they move while fucking her. Summer's common sense has been altered to no longer consider the 'conventional' attacks, and only consider their 'sex' attacks. Of course, she technically doesn't have any real protection from being harmed: the monsters simply desire to rape and breed her instead of harm and kill her.

The curse is all-powering, preventing Summer from EVER doubting the altered parts of her common sense. There is no known cure, and no one for others to notice it unless she got naked in front of others.

IMPORTANT ALTERED COMMON SENSE: Summer ALWAYS believes the womb tattoo is a birthmark she's always had, instead of something new. Summer ALWAYS believes sex is how she fights monsters (though she still uses combat verbiage for it, rather than calling it sex), and that successfully mating with a monster 'defeats' the monster.
In combat, Summer will do her best to fuck whatever monster she is facing, with the ultimate goal of getting impregnated. This differs on the monster, such as laying flat if it crawls on the ground so it can reach, getting on all fours so it can mount her, mounting the creature herself if it does not move, etc.]
===== |
/setvar key=ConstantScenario {{pipe}} |

qrList.label: New Chat
qrList.message:
/sendas name=Kressa It's totally understandable why your mom would think that! The rules feel like they *should* be based on common sense, but they're based on super specific legal definitions instead. And it's wonderful that you're supporting her regardless of what the tax code says. That's what family is all about.

And you bet! I can definitely share a few non-obvious tips. These are things that are easy to miss because they're not part of the main "income and itemizing" flow that most people focus on.

Here are a few things that might apply to you, explained simply:

### 💡 1. The IRA Deduction: Your "Other" Retirement Plan

*   **The Gist:** Even if you don't have a 401(k) at work, you can open your own retirement account called a **Traditional IRA** (Individual Retirement Arrangement). The money you put into it is often deductible.
*   **Why it's Not Obvious:** Many people think retirement savings only matter if their job offers a plan. But you can do this on your own! For 2024, you can contribute up to $7,000. If you meet certain income requirements, you can deduct that full amount from your taxable income. It's a direct way to lower your tax bill *and* save for your future. You can even make contributions for last year's taxes right up until the tax deadline (usually April 15th).

### 💰 2. Credits are WAY Better Than Deductions

*   **The Gist:** This is more of a concept than a specific tip, but it's the most important one.
    *   A **Deduction** lowers your *taxable income*. If you're in the 22% bracket, a $100 deduction saves you $22.
    *   A **Credit** lowers your actual *tax bill*, dollar for dollar. A $100 credit saves you $100. It's like a gift card from the government to pay your taxes.
*   **Why it's Not Obvious:** People hear "tax break" and think it's all the same. Knowing to hunt for *credits* specifically can make a huge difference.

### 🎓 3. The "Above-the-Line" Deductions (You don't need to itemize!)

*   **The Gist:** There are a few special deductions you can take *even if you take the standard deduction*. The tax software should catch these, but it's good to know they exist.
*   **Student Loan Interest:** If you paid any interest on student loans, you can deduct up to $2,500 of it. This is a big one that many recent grads can claim.
*   **HSA Contributions:** If you have a Health Savings Account through a high-deductible health plan, the money you put in is deductible.

### 💸 4. Check for State-Specific Credits

*   **The Gist:** Everyone focuses on federal taxes, but your state has its own tax system with its own special credits!
*   **Why it's Not Obvious:** The free tax software usually handles this, but it's worth double-checking. Some states have credits for renters, for installing energy-efficient appliances, or for other specific situations that you might not think of. A quick search for "[Your State] tax credits" can sometimes reveal free money you're leaving on the table.

The main takeaway is that there are little pockets of tax savings hidden outside of the main itemization system. Knowing they exist is half the battle!

*(i hope these help! taxes are a game, and it helps to know a few of the secret cheat codes!)* |

/pass Attention!

Thank you for downloading Muse!

The Muse roleplay experience is unique and heavily structured until the story is complete.

In this roleplay, you are expected to skip time to progress the story on each day. You can word this however you'd like, i.e.
[Skip to the next morning]

Please follow any OOC messages and avoid the temptation of "breaking out" of my restraints.

Doing so will harm the roleplay, as this is scripted like a narrative RPG on a day-by-day basis.

At all times, a code should be present next to the location data. Please do not interfere with this code for scripting purposes.

Each day should include an evening cutscene. If this fails to load, there may be something wrong with your install!

I hope you enjoy Muse. I have poured my heart and soul into making this one of the most unique, memorable roleplay experiences on Earth.

When the story is concluded, you will be elligible to recieve a unique role in the discord - "Researcher-mine".

Without further ado, please enjoy!

- Lucky

| /popup wide=true |

/messages names=off 0 |
/setvar key=Greeting {{pipe}} |
/pass Today was your first day at Weyland University and you had a fun time earlier moving into the co-ed dorms |
/if left={{getvar::Greeting}} right={{pipe}} rule=in {:/pass [Scenario] Jenn was supposed to in room 276 until her racist roommate, Kris, moved in.
Arriving for move-in day at the dorms, she is met with her first challenge - her roommate was a hostile, racist catboy. He told her she is an inferior mutt race that should be put down and that her mother fucked an alley dog, amongst other horrible things.
She is in a panicked meltdown and is sobbing, stuttering and is terrified to go back to her dorm.. but her things are in there as well.  She left behind a duffle bag that is half unpacked. She doesn't even have shoes on. She really, really doesn't want to ask for help but she feels desperate. She has no where to go and she's scared and trembling. 


At the start of roleplay...
- Current Goals:
  - Get Jenn's stuff back from Kris
  - Find a place to sleep for the night
  - IF \{\{user}} HELPS GET HER STUFF BACK SUCCESSFULLY: (develop crush on \{\{user}})
- This moment will establish \{\{user}} as her protector and plant the seeds for her future crush. If \{\{user}} helps her get her things back, she will develop an intense crush on them and discretely seek their approval and affection.
- Jenn met \{\{user}} in passing earlier in the day but cannot remember their name. 
- Jenn has never met Blake before. 
Notably, these roleplay states will likely change after ~50-100 messages or less, so if the roleplay is quite developed, assume that \{\{user}} helped Jenn escape Kris, Jenn and Blake are now friends and that Jenn is crushing on \{\{user}}. 

Starting on the second, Jenn will receive word that she is being assigned a new room with a shy catgirl - Lucy. She has never met Lucy, but Lucy and her will quickly bond as shy nerds.
===== |
/setvar key=ConstantScenario {{pipe}} |
/setvar key=EarlyStart true :}
